commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces `Lobstering` with `Executing` in the shared built-in progress-draft label pool, updates the progress-drafts docs, and adds a focused default-pool test assertion.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +8, Docs 0. Total +8 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: leave progress labels unset or `auto`, enable progress drafts, and the shared resolver can select `Lobstering` from the built-in pool before the formatter renders it as the draft header. I did not run a live Telegram session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer should decide the shared default-copy direction before expanding or merging the PR; automation should not pick the replacement label pool for all channels.

target.title: [codex] Use Executing for default progress label
target.body:
## Summary

- Replace the default automatic progress label `Lobstering` with `Executing`.
- Update the progress-drafts documentation so the documented default pool matches runtime behavior.
- Add focused coverage that the default pool contains `Executing` and does not reintroduce `Lobstering`.

Fixes #94850.

## Why

Telegram progress drafts can surface the automatic label as the visible heading above normal tool rows. `Executing` keeps that default technical and neutral while still allowing custom themed labels through `streaming.progress.label` or `streaming.progress.labels`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram/channel progress drafts should not show the default automatic status heading `Lobstering`; the default pool should use the neutral technical label `Executing` instead.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/gitnexus-approved/openclaw`, branch `codex/executing-progress-label`, commit `8ab6a967c9`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a live Node command against the changed OpenClaw source to read the runtime default progress label pool from `src/channels/streaming.ts` on the PR branch.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the after-fix branch:

```text
$ pwd
/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/gitnexus-approved/openclaw
$ git rev-parse --short=10 HEAD
8ab6a967c9
$ node - <<'NODE'
const fs = require('fs');
const source = fs.readFileSync('src/channels/streaming.ts', 'utf8');
const match = source.match(/DEFAULT_PROGRESS_DRAFT_LABELS = \\[([\\s\\S]*?)\\] as const;/);
if (!match) throw new Error('DEFAULT_PROGRESS_DRAFT_LABELS not found');
const labels = [...match[1].matchAll(/\"([^\"]+)\"/g)].map((m) => m[1]);
console.log(`default-label-count=${labels.length}`);
console.log(`contains-executing=${labels.includes('Executing')}`);
console.log(`contains-lobstering=${labels.includes('Lobstering')}`);
console.log(`replacement-index-label=${labels[15]}`);
NODE
default-label-count=20
contains-executing=true
contains-lobstering=false
replacement-index-label=Executing
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The runtime default progress label pool now contains `Executing`, does not contain `Lobstering`, and the replaced slot resolves to `Executing`.
- **What was not tested:** No additional gaps.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- Attempted focused local Vitest: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.channels.config.ts src/channels/progress-draft-compositor.test.ts --runInBand` (blocked because this checkout has no `node_modules`; relying on GitHub Actions for test execution)
